purpose: Pure-computation service for dependency-aware dispatch readiness. Does not touch filesystem directly — delegates to DashboardService for init/progress reads. Three exports: getDispatchableTasks(dashboardId) does a full scan returning every agent whose `depends_on` is fully satisfied (all deps have status==='completed') AND whose own progress file does NOT yet exist (i.e., not yet dispatched); computeNewlyUnblocked(dashboardId, completedTaskId) is the optimized incremental variant — only inspects agents whose depends_on includes the just-completed task, used by WatcherService on every progress-completion event to fire 'tasks_unblocked' SSE; getDependencyStatus(dashboardId, taskId) returns per-task aggregate {dependencies map, allSatisfied, satisfiedCount, totalCount} for inspection UI. Status resolution is strict: anything not 'completed' (including 'in_progress', 'failed', 'pending') is treated as unsatisfied.
gotchas: Presence of a progress file = 'task already dispatched/running' regardless of status. A failed task with a progress file is therefore NOT dispatchable via getDispatchableTasks — to retry a failed task you MUST delete its progress file first (this is what !retry / SwarmOrchestrator do). If a progress file gets stuck in failed state and is not deleted, downstream tasks depending on it will never unblock.

computeNewlyUnblocked is fired on EVERY progress write where status==='completed' (in WatcherService) — including spurious re-writes of an already-completed file (master can re-emit). Workers depending on the same parent will receive the 'tasks_unblocked' broadcast multiple times. Listeners must be idempotent.
Status is matched as exact string 'completed'. A worker that writes status: 'complete' (typo) will never satisfy a dependency.

getDependencyStatus returns allSatisfied:true for tasks with NO dependencies AND for unknown task ids (totalCount:0). The two cases are indistinguishable without checking totalCount — caller must handle this when rendering UI.
All three functions wrap in try/catch returning empty/safe defaults — silent failure if init/progress reads throw. Errors do not propagate; debugging requires turning on DashboardService logging.
patterns: Incremental-vs-full scan pair: getDispatchableTasks (O(agents * deps)) for cold start, computeNewlyUnblocked (O(downstream_of_completed)) for hot path — invoked by WatcherService DEPENDENCY_CHECK_DELAY_MS after a completion event.
Output augmentation: returned agent objects are spread copies with an extra `dependency_status` field — original initialization.json data is not mutated.
Helper trio (getAgents, getDependsOn, resolveTaskStatus) defensively handle missing/non-array fields — defends against partial initialization.json writes.
Pending-as-default for missing progress: resolveTaskStatus returns 'pending' for any task without a progress file — this is the correct semantics for unblock checks.
conventions: Pure module — no I/O, no logging, no side effects (file reads happen inside dependencies).
Returns plain JS objects/arrays, no classes.
All public functions wrap their body in try/catch with safe-default returns.
